title: Mover apparatus installed aftermarket or oem for pivoting a cement chute and method
abstract: A mover apparatus having a pinion rigidly attached to a pivot mount connected to a cement chute of a cement mixer and engaged by rack of a mover assembly biased by a guide, which mover assembly, being selectively operated, moves the rack longitudinally between a retracted position and an extended position to impart rotational movement to the pivot mount for swinging the cement chute in an arc relative to the cement mixer for orienting the cement chute to a selected location for discharge of cement. A method of installing a mover apparatus as an aftermarket or OEM structure is disclosed.
claims: Claims Claims  What is claimed is: 1. A mover apparatus for swinging a cement chute pivotably mounted at a discharge of a cement mixer, comprising: a pinion defining a plurality of teeth on a face of the pinion, the pinion for attaching to a pivotable member that connects to a cement chute of a cement mixer; a mover assembly comprising a cylinder for attaching rigidly to the cement mixer and having an extendable shaft for moving between a retracted position and an extended position and an elongated rack that defines a plurality of teeth on a working face and attached at one end to the shaft; a guide for mounting rigidly to a support for the pivotable member for biasing the teeth on the working face in engagement with the teeth of the pinion; and an operation device for operating the cylinder to move the shaft between the retracted and the extended positions selectively, whereby, the pinion engaged to the rack, rotates in response to operation of the cylinder to move between the retracted and extended positions, to swing the cement chute in an arc relative to the cement mixer. 2. The mover apparatus as recited in claim 1, wherein the pinion comprises at least a pair of arcuate members, each having respective opposing ends that abut a respective end of the opposing member for defining a circular ring when attached to the pivotable member. A CMD 2823099 vl 2932560-000002 02/02/2015 3. The mover apparatus as recited in claim 1, wherein the pinion comprises an arcuate member. 4. The mover apparatus as recited in claim 1, wherein the operation device comprises: a valve for communicating with a supply of hydraulic fluid; a pair of hydraulic flow lines for connecting one flow line between the cylinder and the valve and connect another flow line between the cylinder and the supply of hydraulic fluid; and a switch to operate the valve selectively for retracting or extending the shaft. 5. The mover apparatus as recited in claim 1, wherein the guide comprises: a U-channel member that slidingly receives the rack for longitudinal movement during operation of the cylinder; a backer plate that abuts a back face of the U-channel member; a flange extending normal to the backer plate for rigidly attaching to a support for the rotatable member; and connectors that extend through the backer plate and against the U-channel member to bias the teeth of the rack in engagement to the teeth of the pinion. 6. The mover apparatus as recited in claim 5, wherein the backer plate defines a pair of threaded openings; and the connectors comprise a pair of bolts, each for extending threadingly through a respective one of the openings to bear against the U-channel member; and further comprising a pair of lock nuts, each received on a respective one of the bolts for threadingly tightening against the backer plate, for retaining the rack biased in engagement to the pinion. A CMD 2823099 vl 2932560-000002 02/02/2015 7. The mover as recited in claim 1, wherein the cylinder comprises a hydraulic cylinder that communicates through a pump with a hydraulic fluid supply for operating the cylinder to move the member between the retracted position and the extended position. 8. The mover as recited in claim 1, further comprising: a support housing for holding a rotatable shaft to which the pivot mount connects; the pinion rigidly attached to the rotatable shaft within the support housing; a slot defined in a sidewall of the support housing; and the rack received through the slot inwardly of the support housing for engaging the pinion. A CMD 2823099 vl 2932560-000002 02/02/2015 9. A method of retrofitting a manually swung cement chute of a cement mixer with a mover assembly, comprising the steps of: (a) rigidly attaching an arcuate pinion member having a plurality of teeth to a pivot mount that connects to a cement chute of a cement mixer; (b) engaging a rack having a plurality of teeth to the pinion, which rack attaches to a power device that being operated moves the rack longitudinally between an extended position and a retracted position and thereby imparting rotational movement to the pinion; (c) securing the power device to a support on the cement mixer; and (d) connecting the power device to a power source and to an operational controller for selectively moving the rack, wherein the pivot member rotates upon longitudinal movement of the rack during selective operation by the controller. 10. The method as recited in claim 9 further comprising the step of guiding the engagement of the rack and the pinion with a backer plate mounted to the support for biasing the rack into engagement with the pinion. 11. The method as recited in claim 9, wherein the step rigidly attaching comprises attaching a first semicircular member to the pivot mount and attaching a second semicircular member to the pivot mount in opposing relation to the first semicircular member. A CMD 2823099 vl 2932560-000002 02/02/2015
